slides.title: The hidden cost of heating food in *plastic*
slides.sub: Warm plastic can release compounds that quietly interfere with your hormones. A few swaps may help.

slides.title: Heat changes plastic
slides.body: When plastic gets hot, it can leach phthalates and BPA into your food. Heat *speeds up* that transfer.

slides.title: These act on hormones
slides.body: Phthalates and BPA are *endocrine disruptors*. They can mimic or block the hormones that regulate your body.

slides.title: Hormones shape aging
slides.body: This interference is linked to *metabolic and hormonal shifts* tied to aging. Lowering exposure is a sensible hedge.

slides.title: Reheat in glass or steel
slides.body: Move hot food and leftovers into *glass or stainless steel*. Skip microwaving in plastic tubs and takeout containers.

slides.title: Small habits, real margin
slides.body: Let food cool before it touches plastic, and choose *fresh over heavily packaged* when you can. Tiny changes compound.

slides.title: Small swaps, *tracked daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the food, sleep, movement and steps that protect long-term health into one simple daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Reheating dinner in a plastic tub feels harmless. The heat may be doing more than warming your food. 🌿

Warm plastic can leach phthalates and BPA, hormone disruptors linked to metabolic shifts of aging. Swap to glass and steel. Save this. 🔖
